preamble: The transformation of [k] to [dz] involves palatalization followed by voicing, a development often found in specific phonological environments.

examples.note: Transition through palatal stops and subsequent voicing/affrication.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
